Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and seasonal workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. A fatality is a death resulting from a work injury regardless of the time intervening between injury and death.
Please enter the number of work related fatalities for the given year.

Any injury or illness other than death which permanently and totally incapacitates an employee from engaging in any gainful occupation or which results in the loss or the complete loss of use of any of the following in one accident: both eyes; one eye and one hand, or arm, or leg, or foot; any two of the following not in the same limb, hand, arm, foot, leg; permanent complete paralysis of two limbs; or brain injury resulting in incurable imbecility or insanity.
Please enter the number of permanent total disability injuries for the given year.

Any injury or illness other than death or permanent total disability, which results in the loss or loss of use of any member or part of a member of the body regardless of any pre-existing disability of the injured member or impaired body function.
Please enter the number of permanent partial disability injuries for the given year.
Any injury or illness which does not result in death or permanent total or permanent partial disability but which results in disability from work for a day or more.
Please enter the number of temporary total disability injuries for the given year.

“First Aid Treatment” means adequate, immediate and necessary medical and/or dental attendance or remedy given in case of injury or sudden illness suffered by the workers, irrespective of whether or not such illness/injury is occupational in nature, before more extensive medical and/or dental treatment can be secured. It does not include following treatment for an injury or illness. [page 169]
Please enter the number of First Aid Treatments for the given year.

Definitions and requirements as per Rule 1050 [pages 15-19] of the [Philippine Occupational Safety and Health Standards] (as amended) 1) "Total Days Lost" definition in Rule 1051 (9) [page 15] 2) "Scheduled Charges" definition in Rule 1051 (10) [page 15] 3) Refer to Table 6 Tabulation of Scheduled Charges for details [page 192] 4) Time charges or number Total Days Lost on evaluation of disability in Rule 1055.01 [pages 17-19]
Please enter the number of lost working days as a result of workplace accidents and occupational diseases for the given year.

Occupational diseases are officially defined by Philippine law and consist of illnesses recognized as directly caused by work or working conditions. The definitive list of compensable occupational diseases is issued by the Employees' Compensation Commission (ECC), as referenced in the Philippine Labor Code and updated regulations.</br>\n
Please enter the number of occupational illnesses for the given year.

The following are dangerous occurrences, which shall be investigated and reported: a. Explosion of boilers used for heating or power. b. Explosion of a receiver or storage container, with pressure greater than atmospheric, of any gas or gases (including air) or any liquid resulting from the compression of such gases or liquid. c. Bursting of a revolving wheel, grinder stone or grinding wheel operated by mechanical power. d. Collapse of a crane, derrick, winch, hoist or other appliances used in raising or lowering persons or goods or any part thereof, the overturning of a crane, except the breakage of chain or rope sling. e. Explosion or fire causing damage to the structure of any room or place in which persons are employed or to any machine contained therein resulting in the complete suspension of ordinary work in such room or place, or stoppage of machinery or plant for not less than twenty four (24) hours, and f. Electrical short circuit or failure of electrical machinery, plant or apparatus, attended by explosion or fire causing structural damage thereto and involving its stoppage and misuse for not less than 24 hours.
DOLE Bureau of Working Conditions – Official Portal
Occupational Safety and Health Standards (OSHS) – Consolidated Edition by DOLE Philippines
Please enter the number of dangerous occurrences for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
